fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Florida Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Florida - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity florida, how to transfer occupational therapist license to florida, occupational therapist compact florida, florida occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Florida Board of Occupational Therapy Practice
board.parent_dept: Florida Department of Health, Board of Medicine
board.mailing_address:
Florida Department of Health
Board of Occupational Therapy
4052 Bald Cypress Way, Bin C-05
Tallahassee, FL 32399-3255

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Florida.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Register and Apply Online
steps.description: Create an account and complete the initial application for Occupational Therapist licensure via the MQA Online Services portal at FLHealthSource.gov, and pay the non-refundable application fee.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your ACOTE-accredited occupational therapy program to be sent directly from the educational institution to the Florida Board of Occupational Therapy.

steps.title: Submit NBCOT Scores
steps.description: Ensure your official passing scores for the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination are sent directly from NBCOT to the Florida Board of Occupational Therapy.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Undergo a criminal background check by submitting electronic fingerprints through an approved Livescan vendor, which will be submitted to the Florida Department of Law Enforcement (FDLE) and the FBI.

steps.title: Application Review and Approval
steps.description: Allow the Florida Board of Occupational Therapy to review your complete application package for compliance with all licensure requirements before a license is issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Florida OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Florida have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers occupational therapist practice in Florida. To work in Florida, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Florida?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Florida while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in Florida
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Florida, you must use the Florida Department of Health's MQA Online Services portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps:
1. Navigate to FLHealthSource.gov and click 'Apply for a License'.
2. Click 'Register' to create a new user ID and password, or log in if you already have an account.
3. Once logged in, select the option to start a new application for Occupational Therapist licensure.
